a.xlink:title: Core internal event handler reacting to raw descriptor readiness signals.
Core internal event handler reacting to raw descriptor readiness signals.
Immediately releases OpenSSL session pointers and forcefully closes the system-level descriptor.
Intermediate signal hub executed immediately when a secure cryptographic tunnel is fully established.
Immediately releases OpenSSL session pointers and forcefully closes the system-level descriptor.
Low-level system call that checks whether a file descriptor contains data.
Low-level native operating system read operation proxying requests directly down to the fd handle.
Configures or overrides the internal error code.
Updates the human-readable error description buffer string.
Low-level native operating system write operation proxying requests directly up to the fd handle.
Called when the connection state changes.
Closes the network connection and unsubscribes from thread polling events.
Type-safely extracts and returns the stored data value.
Writes raw binary bytes out to the network socket layer.
Called when writing to the socket is possible.
